policy.P0: Core failure that can break transport, live reliability, recording safety or project integrity. Must be resolved before release candidate.
policy.P1: Critical workflow depth or platform support gap that does not fully break the core, but would make Public 1.0 non-credible if left open.
policy.P2: Important quality, depth or workflow improvement that should land for 1.0 if it does not threaten P0/P1 closure.
policy.P3: Polish, convenience or expansion work that is valuable but must never block the release.

P0.title: Finalize recording recovery UX and failure surfacing
definitionOfDone: Finalize partial failures are surfaced deterministically per track/take.
Recovered recording sessions are visible and actionable after restart.
No silent take loss remains in recording smoke and reliability artifacts.

P0.title: Monitor router v2 platform validation
definitionOfDone: Monitoring modes mono/stereo/left/right behave consistently on the target Windows matrix.
Immediate monitor stop works with no residual routed signal.
Monitor latency p95 remains within 12ms at 48k/128.

P0.title: Take lane and comping roundtrip integrity
definitionOfDone: Comp lane rebuild is identical after save/open and crash recovery.
No take, comp segment or lane reference becomes orphaned.
Punch and comp edits remain sample-accurate on renderable audio.

P0.title: Live 48x8 reliability and stage-safe hardening
definitionOfDone: 90 minute live stress run completes without critical failure.
Launch/replay/session recording behavior remains deterministic under stress.
Stage-safe runtime does not surface intrusive technical UX.

P0.title: Windows GA smoke and release discipline
definitionOfDone: Windows smoke matrix is complete and repeatable.
Known P0 and P1 issues are zero at release candidate cut.
Rollback plan and known-issues policy are documented.

P1.title: Routing 1.0 completeness
definitionOfDone: Groups, returns and sends pre/post are stable in large sessions.
Cue monitor path is operational.
Cycle prevention is enforced and tested.

P1.title: Automation runtime depth
definitionOfDone: Read/touch/latch/write are reliable on core parameters.
Automation playback remains outside React hot paths.
Automation does not destabilize Arrange or Session playback.

P1.title: VST3 and CLAP host boundary
definitionOfDone: Plugin scan, cache and quarantine are reproducible.
A faulty plugin cannot crash the whole session without containment.
Plugin crash/fault reporting is retained for support.

P1.title: Export parity and delivery
definitionOfDone: Master and stem export pass parity tolerance checks.
WAV, AIFF, FLAC and MP3 paths are stable.
Export report includes format, duration, peaks and parity notes.

P1.title: Linux Preview packaging and support matrix
definitionOfDone: Linux Preview package strategy is fixed.
Wayland/Hyprland and PipeWire/JACK/ALSA matrix is documented.
Preview limitations are explicit and published.

P2.title: Browser 1.0 workflow depth
definitionOfDone: Import and drag/drop workflows feel complete for 1.0.
Collections/favorites cover the main browse use cases.
Directory scan remains reliable on the Windows GA matrix.

P2.title: MIDI baseline for Public 1.0
definitionOfDone: MIDI input, record and piano roll flow are production-credible.
Quantize and scale helpers cover the baseline writing workflow.
MIDI does not regress transport or recording reliability.

P2.title: Freeze, bounce and commit chain depth
definitionOfDone: Freeze and bounce are stable on representative projects.
Committed chains preserve audible output within tolerance.
Performance gains are measurable after freezing.

P3.title: Expanded diagnostics UX
definitionOfDone: Debug-only diagnostics are more discoverable for QA.
No diagnostic feature leaks into normal end-user UX.

P3.title: Post-1.0 AI and collaboration exploration
definitionOfDone: Any AI or collab work is gated behind 1.0 core health.
No exploratory work can regress transport, recording or release discipline.
